graphProject.name: codex-monorepo
graphProject.description: OpenAI Codex CLI monorepo: Rust agent/runtime crates, TypeScript CLI packaging, SDKs, tooling, documentation, CI, and local wiki materials.

projectMetadata.sourceBaseline: Existing Understand Anything code graph plus derived-knowledge/index.md.

layers.name: Tests, Fixtures, and Snapshots
layers.description: Regression tests, integration suites, fixtures, snapshots, and test support data.

layers.name: Repository Orientation
layers.description: Top-level entry points that explain what the repository is, how it is governed, and how tooling is invoked.

layers.name: Agent Ops and Local Runbooks
layers.description: Codex-local skills, environment recipes, and agent automation guidance used to operate this checkout.

layers.name: Rust Core Agent Runtime
layers.description: Core agent loop, configuration, protocol, model/provider plumbing, backend clients, state, rollout, and common runtime crates.

layers.name: Rust CLI, TUI, and Terminal UX
layers.description: Native command entry points, terminal UI, terminal detection, PTY support, update checks, and user-facing interaction surfaces.

layers.name: App Server and Desktop API
layers.description: App-server daemon, transport, protocol v2 surface, thread storage, and desktop-facing extension APIs.

layers.name: MCP, Tools, Skills, and Connectors
layers.description: Tool-call mediation, MCP clients/servers, plugins, skills, connectors, file search, and hosted tool abstractions.

layers.name: Sandbox, Exec Policy, Security, and Host Integration
layers.description: Shell execution, sandboxing, approval/escalation policy, host hardening, secrets, keyrings, auth, networking, and platform integration.

layers.name: NPM Distribution and Release Packaging
layers.description: JavaScript npm facade, native package layout, release staging scripts, and artifact publication workflows.

layers.name: SDK Runtime and Schema Generation
layers.description: Generated SDK protocol models, Python runtime wheel payloads, and schema/codegen maintenance scripts.

layers.name: Python SDK App-Server Client
layers.description: Python high-level API, stdio JSON-RPC transport, notification router, examples, and tests for app-server integration.

layers.name: TypeScript SDK Exec Client
layers.description: TypeScript API, thread helpers, exec bridge, JSONL event contract, samples, and tests.

layers.name: Install and Distribution Scripts
layers.description: Shell and PowerShell installers plus release asset resolution and checksum plumbing.

layers.name: Rust Quality Tooling
layers.description: Argument-comment lint, Dylint/Bazel integration, and Rust-specific repository quality gates.

layers.name: Other SDKs and External Consumers
layers.description: Remaining external client and sample code that launches, embeds, or talks to Codex.

layers.name: CI and Quality Gates
layers.description: GitHub workflows, reusable actions, code-signing actions, CODEOWNERS, and PR governance.

layers.name: Build, CI, and Repository Tooling
layers.description: Bazel, GitHub automation, devcontainers, repository scripts, third-party support, and maintenance tooling.

layers.name: Public Docs
layers.description: Repository documentation pages that point contributors toward official docs, installation, contribution, and security workflows.

layers.name: Local Knowledge Overlay
layers.description: Local wiki raw materials and structured pages. These are user knowledge-base artifacts layered over the upstream Codex source tree.

layers.name: Documentation and Markdown
layers.description: Remaining markdown and explanatory text that is not already part of public docs or the local wiki.

layers.name: Remaining Source Components
layers.description: Source files and configuration that do not fit the higher-level architectural groupings above.

tour.title: Repository Orientation
tour.description: Start with the user-facing README, agent instructions, root package metadata, and Rust workspace manifest to understand the repository contract.

tour.title: Rust Workspace Shape
tour.description: Inspect how the Cargo workspace fans out into core runtime, protocol, CLI, TUI, app server, tool, and sandbox crates.

tour.title: Agent Core Loop
tour.description: Follow the core session and turn-context files to see how user turns, tools, model providers, and response events are coordinated.

tour.title: Native CLI and TUI
tour.description: Move from the Rust CLI entry point into the terminal UI orchestration and high-touch chat surfaces.

tour.title: NPM Facade and Native Package Layout
tour.description: Understand how the npm package resolves platform-specific native binaries and how release scripts stage that layout.

tour.title: App Server API
tour.description: Read the app-server protocol and daemon/client crates to understand the desktop-facing RPC surface.

tour.title: Tools, MCP, Plugins, and Skills
tour.description: Trace how MCP connections, hosted tools, skills, plugins, and connector abstractions feed the agent tool surface.

tour.title: Sandbox and Execution Policy
tour.description: Inspect command execution, approval/escalation, sandboxing, guardian checks, and host hardening as one policy pipeline.

tour.title: SDKs and Release Automation
tour.description: Connect external SDK execution paths with npm release staging and CI artifact workflows.

tour.title: Documentation and Local Wiki
tour.description: Finish with docs and local wiki material that record operator guidance, architecture notes, and repository learning artifacts.

sections.section: OWASP Agent and LLM Threat Crosswalk

unknowns.note: Current derived-knowledge/index.md contains 93 wikilinks when parsed with [[...]] syntax.
The graph proves structure and file relationships, but historical or product intent should stay marked as needing source validation unless a source file or document states it directly.
OWASP and TC260 taxonomies are available from the user prompt/index plan; no canonical local OWASP/TC260 source document was found in this evidence pass.
local-knowledge-overlay 的原始图谱样例包含编码受损的 wiki/raw 文件名；本 summary 仅保留可读的 wiki 页面样例，原始文件名需要在单独的知识库修复任务中核验。
